methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

data_flows.trust_changes: Minter creates unbacked tokens; no adminSanity or blacklist/pause checks applied
exposure_risks: MASTER_MINTER has unlimited minting with no allowance check
Mint bypasses pause and blacklist checks
manipulation_risks: Compromised MASTER_MINTER can mint unlimited supply
Tokens can be minted to blacklisted addresses

data_flows.trust_changes: Fee deduction occurs before actual transfer via _update; fees bypass pause/blacklist since already checked
validation_points: adminSanity: pause check, blacklist check (bypassed for ADMIN)
exposure_risks: When _feesFaucet == address(0) but _txfeeRate > 0, balance check requires fee+amount but fee is never deducted (phantom fee)
Small transfers produce zero fees due to integer division rounding
manipulation_risks: Admin can set fee rate to 100% (10000), requiring 2x the transfer amount
Rounding-based fee avoidance on small amounts

path: Token._msgSender() extracts req.from from appended calldata
transformations: gasless basefee transferred from payer to paymaster
data_flows.trust_changes: Domain separator is caller-supplied (not contract-derived), enabling potential cross-domain signature replay; trust transitions from signed message to on-chain execution
exposure_risks: Domain separator not validated against contract's own domain - potential cross-chain/cross-contract replay
Forwarder ETH balance drainable via req.value (mitigated by req.to restriction and token lacking receive)
manipulation_risks: Attacker-supplied domainSeparator enables signature portability across deployments
First-time users on multiple chains have same nonce=0, enabling cross-chain replay if same Forwarder address

data_flows.source: Off-chain EIP-712 signature from token holder
path: permit(owner, spender, value, deadline, v, r, s)
data_flows.trust_changes: Off-chain signature authorizes on-chain approval; nonce shared with transferWithAuthorization
exposure_risks: Shared nonce pool with transferWithAuthorization - executing one invalidates the other
No pause or blacklist check on permit
manipulation_risks: Front-running permit with another permit/TWA to consume nonce and grief user
Nonce ordering dependency creates denial-of-service vector for batched authorizations

data_flows.trust_changes: Signed authorization executed by any third party; fees deducted before signature verification
exposure_risks: Fee deduction happens BEFORE signature verification in super call - fees paid even if signature later fails (though nonce increment would cause different hash)
Non-standard EIP-3009 implementation uses sequential nonces instead of random bytes32
manipulation_risks: Anyone can trigger fee deduction from holder by submitting with valid signature
Shared nonce with permit creates invalidation vector

data_flows.trust_changes: A removed controller (no longer CONTROLLER role) can still re-enable operations if they were the one who disabled
manipulation_risks: Removed controller can re-enable operations against admin's wishes

data_flows.trust_changes: No adminSanity checks - bypasses pause and blacklist for basefee payment
exposure_risks: Basefee payment bypasses blacklist and pause checks
Paymaster address (relayer) not validated against blacklist
manipulation_risks: Blacklisted paymaster can receive tokens via basefee
Basefee charged even if contract is paused (via forwarder path)

transformations: OWNER role revoked from member at index 0
data_flows.trust_changes: Brief window where two addresses hold OWNER role between grant and revoke (single tx, not exploitable)
exposure_risks: Relies on EnumerableSet ordering - index 0 assumed to be the old owner
manipulation_risks: If OWNER set somehow has >1 member, revokeRole removes index 0 which may not be the caller

core_rules: MINTER_ROLE admin is MASTER_MINTER; all other role admins are DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE
Transfers require: not paused (unless ADMIN), sender not blacklisted (unless ADMIN), recipient not blacklisted (always), not to contract address
Percentage-based tx fees deducted before transfer, sent to _feesFaucet
Gasless meta-transactions: only transfer() forwarded, basefee paid to relayer post-execution
Safety switch halts minting/burning; re-enabled by original controller or OWNER
economic_incentives: Fee rate configurable 0-100% (0-10000 bps) with FEE_RATIO=10000 denominator
Gasless basefee compensates relayers for gas costs
Minter allowances cap token creation per minter
MASTER_MINTER bypasses allowance entirely - unlimited minting authority
governance_mechanisms: Single OWNER (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE) controls all role assignments and upgrades
ADMIN manages pause, blacklist, fees, forwarder settings
No timelock on any privileged operation
No multi-sig requirement for critical operations
UUPS upgradeable by OWNER with no delay

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fees should either be collected or not required
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Balance check requires fee+amount, but fee is never deducted. Users need excess balance that remains unspent (phantom fee requirement)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Denial of service: users with exact transfer amount in balance cannot transfer even though no fee would actually be taken

edge_cases.scenario: Small transfer amounts with integer division rounding
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All transfers should incur proportional fees
edge_cases.actual_behavior: For txAmount * _txfeeRate < 10000, fee rounds to 0. E.g., 1 unit transfer with 1bps fee = 0 fee
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Splitting large transfers into many small ones to avoid fees entirely

edge_cases.scenario: Shared nonce between permit() and transferWithAuthorization()
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Separate nonce spaces or at least documentation of interaction
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Using _useNonce(owner) in both functions means executing a permit invalidates a pending transferWithAuthorization and vice versa
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Griefing: front-run a transferWithAuthorization with a permit (or vice versa) to invalidate the pending signed transaction

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fee rate should be bounded to reasonable maximum
edge_cases.actual_behavior: 100% fee rate is allowed. Users need 2x the transfer amount (fee equals transfer). Malicious admin can effectively block transfers or extract maximum value
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious ADMIN sets 100% fee rate, every transfer sends equal amount to feesFaucet, effectively doubling cost

edge_cases.scenario: Forwarder domainSeparator is caller-provided, not contract-derived
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Domain separator should be contract-specific to prevent cross-domain replay
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any domain separator is accepted as long as signature recovers to req.from. Cross-chain replay possible for users with same nonce on different chains
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Replay a user's signed ForwardRequest from chain A on chain B where same Forwarder is deployed at same address, if user has same nonce (e.g., both 0 for new user)

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only active controllers or admin should re-enable
edge_cases.actual_behavior: safetySwitch re-enable checks _operatingController == _msgSender(), not the CONTROLLER role. Removed controller retains re-enable authority
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A compromised controller whose role was revoked can still turn operations back on

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Blacklisted addresses should not receive tokens
edge_cases.actual_behavior: mint() does not call adminSanity(); blacklist is not checked. Minter can mint directly to blacklisted addresses
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Compliance bypass: tokens can be created for blacklisted entities via minting

edge_cases.scenario: Minting and burning while contract is paused
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Paused state should halt all token operations
edge_cases.actual_behavior: mint() and burn() only check _operating flag, not paused(). Minting and burning work during pause
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Token supply manipulation continues even when transfers are paused for emergency

edge_cases.scenario: address(0) granted ADMIN and MASTER_MINTER roles during initialization
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Placeholder roles should not create exploitable conditions
edge_cases.actual_behavior: hasRole(ADMIN, address(0)) returns true until setAdministrator is called. If any code path returns address(0) from _msgSender(), ADMIN privileges are obtained
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Theoretical only since _msgSender() cannot practically return address(0) through legitimate paths (ECDSA revert on zero recovery)

edge_cases.actual_behavior: payGaslessBasefee only checks isTrustedForwarder, not paused(). Since Forwarder calls transfer() which checks pause, this only occurs if transfer succeeded. But if ADMIN initiated the meta-tx (bypasses pause), basefee still transfers tokens
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Edge case with ADMIN-as-meta-tx-signer during pause

edge_cases.actual_behavior: burn(0) succeeds and emits Burn event with 0 amount. mint(0) reverts with InvalidAmount

edge_cases.expected_behavior: MASTER_MINTER should have unlimited minting regardless
edge_cases.actual_behavior: mint() first checks if caller has MASTER_MINTER (passes), then checks if caller has MINTER_ROLE - if yes, applies allowance limit. So a dual-role holder is limited by minter allowance
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Unexpected restriction on MASTER_MINTER if they were also granted MINTER_ROLE

assumption_violations: setTxFeeRate and setGaslessBasefee contain dead code: uint256 can never be < 0, making the checks 'newTxFeeRate < 0' and 'newGaslessBasefee < 0' always false
mint() uses 'amount <= 0' check but uint256 cannot be negative, so this is effectively 'amount == 0'
Forwarder assumes domainSeparator is honest (caller-provided), violating EIP-712's intent of domain binding
Missing __gap storage slots in ERC20ControlerMinterUpgradeable, ERC20AdminUpgradeable, ERC20MetaTxUpgradeable, and FeesHandlerUpgradeable risk storage collisions on upgrade
EnumerableSet ordering assumption in setOwner: always assumes current owner is at index 0
TransferWithAuthorization type hash uses 'owner' field name but function parameter is 'holder' - inconsistency may confuse integrators
game_theory_risks: Fee avoidance via dust transfers: splitting large transfers into many sub-threshold amounts to round fees to zero
Nonce griefing: front-run a signed transferWithAuthorization by executing a permit for the same owner (consuming their nonce, invalidating the TWA)
Admin centralization: single ADMIN can set 100% fee rate, pause at will, blacklist arbitrarily, force transfer - no timelock or multi-sig
Forwarder relayer MEV: relayer can sandwich the forwarded transfer with their own trades since they control execution timing
Cross-chain signature replay via Forwarder's unchecked domain separator
integration_risks: UUPS upgrade has no timelock - owner can immediately change implementation, potentially rugpulling all holders
Forwarder is a separate contract (not upgradeable via same proxy) - must be redeployed separately; if Forwarder has bugs, trusted forwarder must be re-set
No ERC20 return value issues since built on OZ ERC20, but integrators must account for fee-on-transfer behavior
EIP-2612 permit implementation uses custom nonce scheme (not OZ standard Nonces.sol) which may conflict with off-chain tooling expectations
Non-standard EIP-3009 implementation: sequential nonces, different type hash field names, shared nonce pool
calculation_errors: Fee calculation (txAmount * _txfeeRate) / FEE_RATIO truncates toward zero, creating fee-free zones for small transactions. With 6 decimals and min fee rate of 1bps, transfers under 10000 units (0.01 EURF) pay zero fees
No overflow risk in fee calculation with uint256 but precision loss is systematic
payGaslessBasefee has no overflow concern since _gaslessBasefee is admin-set, but it's not bounded by any maximum - admin could set it to drain user balance

token_semantics.notes: Stablecoin with admin-configurable percentage fees (0-100%). Fee deducted BEFORE transfer via separate _update call to feesFaucet. Integrators must account for fee deduction reducing sender balance prior to transfer. forceTransfer allows ADMIN clawback. Uses custom EIP-2612 and EIP-3009 with shared sequential nonces. When feesFaucet is address(0), phantom balance requirement exists (fee checked but not deducted).

invariants.name: supply_conservation
invariants.rule: totalSupply == sum of all balanceOf at all times; minting increases both equally, burning decreases both equally, transfers and fees redistribute without changing total

invariants.name: minter_allowance_monotonic_decrease
invariants.rule: For MINTER_ROLE, minterAllowed decreases on each mint and is only increased by MASTER_MINTER via updateMintingAllowance or addMinter

invariants.name: single_owner
invariants.rule: Exactly 1 member should hold DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE at steady state; transiently 2 during setOwner execution

invariants.name: operating_controller_consistency
invariants.rule: _operating == true implies _operatingController == address(0); _operating == false implies _operatingController != address(0)

invariants.name: fee_balance_sufficiency
invariants.rule: balanceOf[sender] >= calculateTxFee(amount) + amount must hold for any transfer when _txfeeRate > 0

invariants.name: nonce_monotonic_increase
invariants.rule: Nonce for each address strictly increases by 1 on each permit() or transferWithAuthorization() call involving that address as signer
